en-US.name: Dog Breeding: Ovulation Timing
en-US.subtitle: Heat Cycle & Whelping Planner
en-US.promotional_text: Dog breeder? Enter your bitch's heat date and progesterone readings to get her ovulation day, breeding windows by semen type, and whelping due date.
en-US.description:
Timing is the hardest part of dog breeding. Miss the window and you wait months for the next cycle. Dog Breeding: Ovulation Timing turns your dog's progesterone readings into one clear plan - when she ovulates, when to breed, and when she is due.

Every breeder knows the frustration: a single progesterone number tells you almost nothing, labs report different units, and the fertile window shifts by breed, by dog, and even between cycles. Frozen semen needs a 12-hour window; natural breeding is far more forgiving. Getting it wrong costs a whole season.

HOW IT WORKS
1. Enter your bitch's heat start date, breed size, and semen type.
2. Add each progesterone reading as you get it - in ng/mL or nmol/L, the app converts automatically.
3. You get her ovulation day, the breeding window for your semen type, the recommended re-test day, and the whelping due date.
It takes seconds to set up. No account required. Works fully offline.

CORE FEATURES

Ovulation Verdict
Enter a reading and see the cycle phase, the estimated ovulation day, and the reason behind it - not just a raw number.

Unit Conversion
Log values in ng/mL or nmol/L. The app normalizes both so a reading from any analyzer reads correctly.

Natural Breeding Window
See the fertile days for natural mating, with the ideal days highlighted.

PRO FEATURES
Upgrade to Pro for deeper insights:
- Track multiple bitches at once
- Breeding windows for chilled and frozen semen
- Breed-size ovulation curve model
- Full progesterone trend chart and cycle history
- Re-test day reminders
- Whelping due-date countdown with milestones

PRIVACY FIRST
- No account, no login
- All data stored locally on your device
- No location, no network, no tracking, no ads

Dog Breeding: Ovulation Timing is an informational planning tool for breeders based on general canine reproduction references. Progesterone thresholds and units vary by laboratory and analyzer. It is not a substitute for advice from a licensed veterinarian.
